Description: Exterior surfaces not inherently resistant to decay must be treated with a protective coating (paint). Violation occurs when paint is peeling, flaking, or chipped, often indicating a lead hazard.
Prohibits parking any motor vehicle on a lawn, dirt, or any unpaved surface in the front or side yard of a residential property.
No inoperative or unlicensed motor vehicle shall be parked, kept, or stored on any premises. This includes cars with flat tires, missing parts, or expired tags.
Grass, weeds, or uncultivated plant growth must not exceed 10 inches in height. This includes "noxious weeds" and rank vegetable growth.
Roofs must be structurally sound and weather-tight. Violations include visible holes, the use of temporary tarps as a long-term fix, or heavy discoloration indicating rot or leaks.
All window glass (glazing) must be free from cracks and holes. Windows must be weather-tight and capable of being held in position by hardware.
Exterior doors and hardware must be in good condition, fit strictly within the frame to be weather-tight, and be capable of being locked.
The accumulation of waste, trash, or debris (scrap wood, metal, tires, household junk) on the exterior property or within the structure.
Sidewalks, walkways, and driveways must be kept in a proper state of repair and free from hazardous conditions like deep cracks, buckling, or crumbling.
Exterior walls and trim must be free from holes, breaks, and loose or rotting materials. Siding must be properly attached to prevent weather entry.
Foundation walls must be maintained in good repair, plumb (vertical), and free from open cracks or breaks that could admit rodents or water.
Every exterior stairway, porch, and balcony must be structurally sound, in good repair, and capable of supporting the weight of people using them.
A structure that is vacant and unsecured (open at doors/windows) or is so damaged/dilapidated that it is determined to be dangerous to human life.
